content.claim: Recorded SEN prevalence varies dramatically by county, ranging from 15.6% of students in Békés county to 6.2% in Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén county.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Researchers explicitly caution that regional differences may reflect diagnostic and support-system quality/access rather than true underlying need variation

Low recorded prevalence does not definitively indicate fewer SNI children; it may indicate under-identification due to service gaps, making raw prevalence rates unsuitable as sole planning demand metrics
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
